source.multipart_basis: Decomposition reflects the tubular efficiency-EV chassis described in the original brief: main-frame longitudinal tubes, roll bar, seat-back mount, pedal-box bulkhead, steering column support, battery tray, rear-wheel axle plate, and fairing attachment points as separate bodies. No rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Deliver the primary tubular roll-cage space frame of a single-seat Elektrikli (Electric) vehicle competing in the 2025 Efficiency Challenge as a multi-body assembly rather than a single solid body. The complete car including driver (75 kg reference) and battery pack (15 kg) has a dry curb weight below 180 kg. The chassis is seamless AISI 4130 chromoly (Young's modulus 205 GPa, yield 460 MPa, UTS 560 MPa, rho 7850 kg/m^3); main-hoop and front-hoop tubes are OD 25.4 mm x wall 2.4 mm, secondary bracing is OD 22.2 mm x wall 1.65 mm. Frame envelope is 2.6 m (L) x 1.3 m (W) x 1.1 m (H) with 50 mm helmet clearance. A separately-evaluated crash-box impact attenuator bolts to the front bulkhead (7350 J absorb, <= 40 g transmitted; out of scope for linear FEA). Load cases (unchanged): (LC1) front impact 7 g decel on 180 kg at front bulkhead; (LC2) 20 kN lateral at driver-cell side-impact bar; (LC3) rear impact 6 g decel on 180 kg at rear bulkhead; (LC4) rollover 2650 N vertical down at main-hoop apex with four wheel pads pinned; (LC5) torsion 800 N up at one front wheel pad + 800 N down at diagonally opposite rear wheel pad, other two pinned. Pass/fail criteria (unchanged): (R1) max von Mises <= 306 MPa (460 / 1.5) across every LC per FIA Appendix J Article 253 conservative gate; (R2) main-hoop apex deflection <= 25 mm under LC4; (R3) torsional stiffness from LC5 >= 2500 N*m/deg; (R4) frame dry mass (tubes + gussets + bulkhead plates, no crash box) <= 45 kg; (R5) first global torsional mode >= 35 Hz. In this multi-part rendering the chassis is decomposed into the following design-specific bodies: (A) main frame longitudinal tubes (15 tube segments; OD 25.4 mm x 2.4 mm and 22.2 mm x 1.65 mm per position), (B) roll bar / main hoop (1 main hoop plus optional front hoop = 2 hoops total), (C) seat-back mount (1 tubular cross-brace + bracket plate behind driver), (D) pedal-box bulkhead (1 plate + frame closing the footwell), (E) steering column support (1 tubular bracket), (F) battery tray (1 welded subframe carrying the 15 kg pack), (G) rear-wheel axle plate (2 plates, left/right, anchoring rear suspension pickups), (H) fairing attachment points (4 to 8 small tabs welded to outer tubes). Interfaces: all tube-to-tube joints at nodes are welded; battery tray is bolted to lower longitudinals; axle plates are bolted and welded; pedal-box bulkhead and seat-back mount are welded to cage members; fairing tabs are welded to outer tubes. Final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED chassis; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection DCR must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case. All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criterion R_asm1 is added. Deliverables: STEP AP242 assembly with each tube segment and plate as a separate body, weld pattern schedule, FEA stress per load case over the assembled frame, torsional-stiffness computation from LC5, modal report, mass budget, and per-connection DCR table.
short_prompt:
Deliver the primary tubular roll-cage space frame of a single-seat Elektrikli (Electric) vehicle competing in the 2025 Efficiency Challenge as a multi-body assembly.

Envelope: frame 2.6 m (L) x 1.3 m (W) x 1.1 m (H) with 50 mm helmet clearance. Complete car including 75 kg driver and 15 kg battery pack has dry curb weight below 180 kg. Seamless AISI 4130 chromoly (yield 460 MPa, UTS 560 MPa); main/front hoops OD 25.4 mm x wall 2.4 mm, secondary bracing OD 22.2 mm x wall 1.65 mm. Crash-box attenuator bolts to front bulkhead (7350 J absorb, <= 40 g transmitted; out of scope for linear FEA).

Load cases: (LC1) front impact 7 g decel on 180 kg at front bulkhead; (LC2) 20 kN lateral at driver-cell side-impact bar; (LC3) rear impact 6 g decel on 180 kg at rear bulkhead; (LC4) rollover 2650 N vertical down at main-hoop apex, four wheel pads pinned; (LC5) torsion 800 N up at one front wheel pad + 800 N down at diagonally opposite rear pad.

Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises <= 306 MPa (460/1.5) across every LC per FIA Appendix J Article 253 conservative gate; (R2) main-hoop apex deflection <= 25 mm under LC4; (R3) torsional stiffness from LC5 >= 2500 N*m/deg; (R4) frame dry mass <= 45 kg; (R5) first global torsional mode >= 35 Hz.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Crash-box 7350 J energy absorption with <= 40 g transmitted deceleration is handled by explicit-dynamics, not linear FEA. Flagged as separate submission.

prompt.title: 2025 Efficiency Challenge Electric Vehicle Tubular Chassis (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: AISI 4130 chromoly tubular chassis for <180 kg EV, 2.6 x 1.3 x 1.1 m envelope. Multi-part: 15 main frame tube segments + 2 hoops + seat-back mount + pedal-box bulkhead + steering column support + battery tray + 2 rear-axle plates + fairing tabs. FIA Appendix J Article 253 conservative stress and rollover gates preserved; FEM on assembled chassis, connection DCR <= 1.0.

load_cases.name: front_impact_7g
load_cases.application: 180 kg decel at front bulkhead / pedal-box

load_cases.name: rear_impact_6g
load_cases.application: 180 kg decel at rear bulkhead / axle plates

load_cases.name: rollover
load_cases.application: main-hoop apex downward, four wheel pads pinned

load_cases.name: torsion
load_cases.application: diagonal wheel-pad couple 800 N up / 800 N down

parts.name: main_frame_longitudinal_tube
parts.role: primary chassis tube segments forming upper and lower longitudinals and cross-members of the driver cell
parts.count_note: ~15 tube segments between nodes is typical for a single-seat efficiency EV space frame

parts.name: roll_bar
parts.role: main hoop (and optional front hoop) carrying LC4 rollover load at apex
parts.count_note: 1 main hoop required; front hoop typically adds a second instance

geometric_constraints.shape: U-bent seamless tube from floor to apex to floor

parts.name: seat_back_mount
parts.role: tubular cross-brace + bracket behind driver supporting seat back and harness anchor

parts.name: pedal_box_bulkhead
parts.role: plate + frame closing the footwell, reacts LC1 front-impact decel into longitudinals

parts.name: steering_column_support
parts.role: tubular bracket reacting steering column loads into upper longitudinals

parts.name: battery_tray
parts.role: welded subframe carrying the 15 kg battery pack, bolted to lower longitudinals
parts.material_hint: Al 6061 or 4130 tubular frame with sheet floor

parts.name: rear_wheel_axle_plate
parts.role: left/right mounting plates anchoring rear suspension pickups into rear bulkhead tubes; reacts LC3 and LC5

parts.name: fairing_attachment_tab
parts.role: small tabs welded to outer tubes providing bolt-down points for external aero fairing (not structural load path)
parts.count_note: Typical efficiency-EV fairing takes 4-8 tabs; exact count team-dependent

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 2.6 m x 1.3 m x 1.1 m with 50 mm helmet clearance
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: x longitudinal (rear +), y lateral (port +), z vertical (up +); origin at driver hip-point floor projection

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: 460 / 1.5 yield FoS per FIA Appendix J Article 253 conservative gate

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all welded and bolted inter-part interfaces (IF1..IF8) <= 1.0 under governing load case (LC4 rollover for apex path; LC2 side-impact for driver cell).

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam (BEAM188) with circular tube section for A/B/C/E/H; shell for plates D/F/G; solid TET10 at welded nodes for local refinement

interfaces.load_path: tube-to-tube nodal welds transferring longitudinal/cross-member loads throughout space frame
main/front hoop feet welded to lower longitudinals; LC4 rollover flows through this joint
seat-back cross-brace welded to upper longitudinals
pedal-box bulkhead frame welded to front longitudinals; reacts LC1 front-impact decel
steering column support welded to upper longitudinals at dashboard station
battery tray bolted down to lower longitudinals via threaded inserts; removable pack
rear-wheel axle plates welded along one edge and bolted along the pickup flange; reacts LC3 and LC5
fairing tabs welded to outer tubes; non-primary load path but reports DCR

fem_check_policy.rationale: Tube-node stiffness is strongly coupled across the entire cage; torsional stiffness (R3) and first torsion mode (R5) are only meaningful on the assembled frame. Per-part stress is reported for visibility but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Original brief explicitly asks for each tube section as a separate body plus weld-pattern schedule; the chassis cannot be modelled as a single solid without collapsing the welded-node stiffness that governs torsional stiffness and rollover deflection gates.
notes.speculation_caveats: Tube-segment count (15), front-hoop presence, and fairing-tab count (4-8) are speculative; the main hoop, seat-back mount, pedal-box bulkhead, steering column support, battery tray, and rear-axle plates are explicitly named in the original design intent.
